decision_policy.adoption_style: Alex adopts quickly once the path is concrete, but only after bounding downside risk.
rejection_triggers: another dashboard without reducing current complexity
setup that depends on perfect data hygiene
vague AI claims without clear controls or review steps
team workflow changes that require broad buy-in before any individual value appears
proof_requirements: a short demo tied to coordination work similar to Alex's
before-and-after evidence on follow-up clarity or time saved
a low-risk pilot using non-sensitive or lightly sensitive data
signals that other practical users, not only early adopters, made it stick
screening_rules: must replace or simplify an existing habit within days, not months
must be explainable to a manager or teammate in a few sentences
must show what data is used and why
decision_policy.pricing_logic: Upper-middle income does not erase cost scrutiny; recurring spend is judged against contract volatility and whether the tool replaces manual work or duplicate subscriptions.
decision_policy.pilot_boundary: Alex is willing to test on a narrow live workflow first, then expand only if the tool survives a busy week without creating cleanup debt.

response_style.preferred_tone_from_others: plainspoken, concrete, and respectful of time
best_message_structure: give a small next step instead of a full rollout
response_style.skepticism_pattern: Alex rarely argues with marketing claims directly; instead, questions whether the product will survive real exceptions and mixed-team habits.

audit.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

llm_enrichment.rationale: {'enrichment_strategy': 'Added realistic operational tension, procurement constraints, and household-driven time fragmentation while preserving fixed identity and seed facts.', 'fixed_facts_preserved': ['Alex Chan, 54, non-binary, in Shatin', 'single adult in a multi-generational household', 'program manager with postgraduate education', 'upper-middle income with contract-based employment and volatile cash flow', 'Cantonese and English speaker', 'tool-heavy workflow, high schedule pressure, medium willingness to change'], 'anti_stereotype_guardrails': ['household structure is treated as a time and logistics factor, not a cultural stereotype', 'gender identity is reflected through inclusion expectations, not deterministic behavior', 'income band is balanced against cash-flow volatility so purchasing caution remains plausible'], 'product_validation_uses': ['evaluate onboarding friction', 'test proof formats for mainstream but busy buyers', 'stress-test privacy messaging and low-risk pilot design', 'assess whether a product truly replaces existing coordination work']}
